Description: Set up the base project structure, dependencies, configuration files, and development workflow for the AI Resume Intelligence Dashboard.
Build the modern SaaS-style frontend interface using Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, and reusable UI components.
Implement user signup, login, logout, and authenticated navigation flow.
Allow users to paste resume text or upload a text-based PDF resume for analysis.
Implement lightweight rule-based resume analysis for ATS score, skills, missing skills, strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations.
Store users, resumes, and analysis reports using Prisma ORM and PostgreSQL or Neon database.
Display score cards, charts, detected skills, recommendations, and previous analysis history on the dashboard.
Verify core flows, fix demo blockers, prepare project for college presentation and SCM review.
Create the base Next.js App Router project with TypeScript configuration and required folder structure.
Install Tailwind CSS, Lucide icons, Recharts, and required frontend dependencies.
Organize app, components, lib, prisma, and types folders according to the project architecture.
Create .env.example for DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET, and local development configuration.
Document project overview, installation steps, database setup, and run commands.
As a visitor, I want to see a professional landing page so that I understand the purpose of the resume dashboard.
Create reusable Button, Card, Input, Textarea, Badge, Label, and Progress components for consistent UI.
As a user, I want a navigation header so that I can move between landing, dashboard, profile, login, and logout actions.
Create global CSS for dark theme, glassmorphism cards, typography, background, and responsive layout behavior.
As a logged-in user, I want a dashboard layout so that I can submit my resume and view analysis results in one place.
As a new user, I want to create an account using name, email, and password so that my resume reports can be saved.
As an existing user, I want to log in with email and password so that I can access my dashboard.

Summary: Show authentication loading and error states
Description: Display loading spinner and readable error messages during login and signup actions.
As a logged-in user, I want to log out from the navigation bar so that I can end my session safely.

Summary: Redirect unauthenticated users from protected pages
Description: Ensure dashboard and profile pages redirect unauthenticated users to the login page.
As a user, I want to paste my resume text so that I can analyze it without uploading a file.
As a user, I want to upload a text-based PDF resume so that the system can extract and analyze the resume content.
Add a button that fills the resume textarea with sample resume content for quick demo testing.
Disable the Generate Analysis button and show a spinner while the resume is being analyzed.
Display user-friendly error messages for short text, invalid PDF, or failed analysis requests.
Create frontend, backend, devops, programming, AI, and tools skill categories for the rule-based analyzer.
As a user, I want the system to detect skills from my resume so that I can understand what technologies are visible.
As a user, I want to see missing industry skills so that I know what to improve in my resume.
As a user, I want an ATS-style score so that I can estimate how strong my resume appears.
As a user, I want strengths and weaknesses so that I can understand my resume quality clearly.
As a user, I want actionable recommendations so that I can improve my resume before applying.
Define User, Resume, and AnalysisReport models with relationships in Prisma schema.

Summary: Configure PostgreSQL or Neon database connection
Description: Connect the application to PostgreSQL locally or Neon using DATABASE_URL environment variable.
Create a reusable Prisma client singleton for database access across API routes.
As a user, I want submitted resumes to be saved so that I can view them later.
As a user, I want generated analysis reports to be saved so that my dashboard history is persistent.
As a user, I want to see ATS, frontend, backend, and industry readiness scores as cards so that I can quickly understand my resume performance.
As a user, I want to see skill distribution by category so that I understand my technical profile visually.
As a user, I want to compare all resume scores visually so that I can identify strong and weak areas.
As a user, I want structured feedback sections so that I can easily act on the resume analysis.

Summary: Display detected and missing skills as badges
Description: As a user, I want detected and missing skills shown as badges so that the result is easy to scan.
As a user, I want to view previous analysis reports so that I can compare or revisit older resume submissions.
As a user, I want a profile page showing my account details and uploaded resumes so that I can review my activity.
Run the Next.js production build to verify the application compiles successfully.
Verify user signup, login, protected dashboard access, and logout behavior before demo.
Verify that pasted resume text generates scores, skills, recommendations, and saved history.
Verify that text-based PDFs work and scanned PDFs show a helpful fallback message.
Prepare a short explanation of project intro, problem statement, frontend build, and dashboard flow.
Check landing page, auth pages, dashboard, charts, and profile page on desktop and smaller screen sizes.
